---
title: "Infection_analysis"
author: "JK"
date: "2025-03-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#load packages
library("tidyverse")
library("ggplot2")
library("tidyr")
library("dplyr")
library("cowplot")
library("chisq.posthoc.test")
library("RVAideMemoire")
library("stats")
library("ggsignif")
library("lme4")
library("forcats")
library("emmeans")
library("patchwork")
library("performance")
library("lattice")
library("car")
library("grid")
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(survival)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)
library(survival)
library(ggplot2)
library(survminer)
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
#set working directory
shapes = c("ECAH" = 21, "ECG" = 22, "ORAPG"=24)
colvec<- c("control" = "grey1","infected" = "goldenrod1")
# Apply the custom function to create a new column with color codes
```


```{r}
loss<-read.csv("Infection_tissue_loss.csv")#%>%
# dplyr::select(-g,-b)
str(loss)
#make na new column called algae type to code if genotype host cladocopium, or durisdinium
loss <- loss %>%
  mutate(algae_type = case_when(
    gen == "ECAH" ~ "Cladocopium",
    gen == "ECG" ~ "Durisdinium",
    gen == "ORAPG" ~ "Durisdinium",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Assigns NA to cases where none of the above conditions are met
  ))
loss <- loss[loss$gen != "ORAG", ]
loss$day<-as_factor(loss$day)
loss$treatment<-as_factor(loss$treatment)
loss$genotype<-as_factor(loss$gen)
loss$treatment<-as_factor(loss$treatment)
loss$algae_type<-as_factor(loss$algae_type)
#filter day 7 only
loss_final<- loss %>% filter(day == 7)
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(readr)

shapes <- c("ECAH" = 21, "ECG" = 22, "ORAPG" = 24)
colvec <- c("control" = "grey40", "infected" = "goldenrod1")

loss <- read.csv("Infection_tissue_loss.csv")

str(loss)

# create algae type
loss <- loss %>%
  mutate(
    algae_type = case_when(
      gen == "ECAH"  ~ "Cladocopium",
      gen == "ECG"   ~ "Durusdinium",
      gen == "ORAPG" ~ "Durusdinium",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# remove unwanted genotype if needed
loss <- loss %>%
  filter(gen != "ORAG")

# make variables factors
loss <- loss %>%
  mutate(
    day = as.factor(day),
    treatment = as.factor(treatment),
    genotype = as.factor(gen),
    algae_type = as.factor(algae_type)
  )

# filter day 7 only
loss_final <- loss %>%
  filter(day == "7")

# summarize for bar plot
loss_summary <- loss_final %>%
  group_by(algae_type, treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_loss = mean(percent_tissue_loss, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_loss = sd(percent_tissue_loss, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(percent_tissue_loss)),
    se_loss = sd_loss / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)

sym_cols <- c(Cladocopium = "brown4", Durusdinium = "darkgreen")
bg_cols  <- c(control = "gray40", infected = "goldenrod1")
shapes   <- c("ECAH" = 21, "ECG" = 22, "ORAPG" = 24)

loss <- read.csv("Infection_tissue_loss.csv")

loss <- loss %>%
  mutate(
    algae_type = case_when(
      gen == "ECAH"  ~ "Cladocopium",
      gen == "ECG"   ~ "Durusdinium",
      gen == "ORAPG" ~ "Durusdinium",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(gen != "ORAG") %>%
  mutate(
    day = as.factor(day),
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("control", "infected")),
    gen = factor(gen, levels = c("ECAH", "ECG", "ORAPG")),
    algae_type = factor(algae_type, levels = c("Cladocopium", "Durusdinium"))
  )

loss_final <- loss %>%
  filter(day == "7")

tissue_loss_fin <- ggplot(loss_final, aes(x = treatment, y = percent_tissue_loss)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 1.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = bg_cols["control"], alpha = 0.10) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 1.5, xmax = 2.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = bg_cols["infected"], alpha = 0.30) +
  geom_boxplot(
    aes(fill = algae_type, group = interaction(treatment, gen)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    outlier.shape = NA,
    width = 0.6,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = algae_type, shape = gen),
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.5, dodge.width = 0.9),
    size = 4,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sym_cols) +
  scale_color_manual(values = sym_cols) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = shapes) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Treatment",
    y = "Percent tissue loss",
    fill = "Symbiont",
    color = "Symbiont",
    shape = "Genotype"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1)
  )
pdf("tissue_loss.pdf", height = 4, width = 6)
print(tissue_loss_fin)
dev.off()
```




```{r}
losslm <-lm(percent_tissue_loss~treatment*gen, data = loss_final)
Anova(losslm)
```

```{r}
emmeans(losslm, list(pairwise ~ treatment+gen), adjust = "tukey")
```

```{r}
losslm <-lm(percent_tissue_loss~treatment*algae_type*day, data = loss)
Anova(losslm)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

sym_cols <- c(Cladocopium = "brown4", Durusdinium = "darkgreen")
bg_cols  <- c(control = "gray40", infected = "goldenrod1")
colvec   <- c(control = "gray40", infected = "goldenrod1")
shapes   <- c("ECAH" = 21, "ECG" = 22, "ORAPG" = 24)

loss <- read.csv("Infection_tissue_loss.csv")

loss <- loss %>%
  mutate(
    algae_type = case_when(
      gen == "ECAH"  ~ "Cladocopium",
      gen == "ECG"   ~ "Durusdinium",
      gen == "ORAPG" ~ "Durusdinium",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(gen != "ORAG") %>%
  mutate(
    day = factor(day),
    day_num = as.numeric(as.character(day)),
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("control", "infected")),
    gen = factor(gen, levels = c("ECAH", "ECG", "ORAPG")),
    algae_type = factor(algae_type, levels = c("Cladocopium", "Durusdinium"))
  )

tissue_loss_all <- ggplot(loss, aes(x = day, y = percent_tissue_loss)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 1.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = bg_cols["control"], alpha = 0.10) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 1.5, xmax = 2.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = bg_cols["infected"], alpha = 0.30) +
  geom_boxplot(
    aes(fill = algae_type, group = interaction(treatment, gen)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    outlier.shape = NA,
    width = 0.6,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = algae_type, color = algae_type, shape = gen),
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.08, dodge.width = 0.8),
    size = 4,
    stroke = 1,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sym_cols) +
  scale_color_manual(values = sym_cols) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = shapes) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Treatment",
    y = "Percent tissue loss",
    fill = "Symbiont",
    color = "Symbiont",
    shape = "Genotype"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1)
  )+facet_wrap(~algae_type)

tissue_loss_all
tissue_loss_all
```



```{r}
emmeans(losslm, list(pairwise ~ treatment+algae_type|day), adjust = "tukey")
```


```{r}
infect <- read.csv("Infection_mortality.csv", header=T)
str(infect)
infect <- infect %>%
  mutate(gen = case_when(
    gen == "Gen1:C" ~ "ECAH",
    gen == "Gen4:C" ~ "ECG",
    gen == "Gen4:D" ~ "ORAPG",
    TRUE ~ gen  # Keeps original values for all other cases
  ))
#make na new column called algae type to code if genotype host cladocopium, or durisdinium
infect <- infect %>%
  mutate(algae_type = case_when(
    gen == "ECAH" ~ "Cladocopium",
    gen == "ECG" ~ "Durisdinium",
    gen == "ORAPG" ~ "Durisdinium",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Assigns NA to cases where none of the above conditions are met
  ))

# Load necessary library
library(survival)

# Load necessary library
library(survival)

# Create an interaction term in the dataset
infect$treat_algae_interact <- interaction(infect$treatment, infect$gen)

# Prepare the survival fit object using the Surv function to create a survival object from your data
# Now using the new interaction term 'treat_algae_interact' in the model
infect_sfit <- survfit(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treat_algae_interact, data = infect)

# Display the summary table of the survival fit object
summary(infect_sfit)$table

# Create separate survfit objects for treatment and algae_type for more detailed faceting
infect_sfit_treatment <- survfit(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treatment, data = infect)
infect_sfit_algae_type <- survfit(Surv(day, mortality) ~ algae_type, data = infect)
```

```{r}
# Cox model
str(infect)
cox_poc <- coxph(Surv(day, mortality) ~treat_algae_interact, data = infect)
summary(cox_poc)

# Simplified model without interaction
simple_cox <- coxph(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treatment * gen, data = infect)
summary(simple_cox)

# Check for zero or near-zero variance predictors
library(caret)
nearZeroVar(infect)

# Examine the number of events per group
table(infect$treatment, infect$mortality)
table(infect$algae_type, infect$mortality)

# If interaction is essential, consider stratification as an alternative approach
strat_cox <- coxph(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treatment + strata(algae_type), data = infect)
summary(strat_cox)

```

```{r}
# Plotting the survival curves for treatment, faceted by algae_type
ggsurvplot(
  infect_sfit_treatment,       # Use the treatment survfit object
  data = infect,
  pval = TRUE,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  palette = c("grey1", "goldenrod1"),  # Adjust based on the number of treatments
  xlab = "Days",
  ylab = "Survival Probability",
  facet.by = "algae_type",     # Faceting by algae type
  facet.scales = "free_y"      # Allows different y scales for each facet; use "fixed" if you want the same scale
)
ggsave("survival_plot.pdf", ,height = 4,width = 6)
dev.off
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(ggplot2)
library(caret)

infect <- read.csv("Infection_mortality.csv", header = TRUE)
str(infect)

infect <- infect %>%
  mutate(
    gen = case_when(
      gen == "Gen1:C" ~ "ECAH",
      gen == "Gen4:C" ~ "ECG",
      gen == "Gen4:D" ~ "ORAPG",
      TRUE ~ gen
    )
  )

# make a new column called algae_type
infect <- infect %>%
  mutate(
    algae_type = case_when(
      gen == "ECAH"  ~ "Cladocopium",
      gen == "ECG"   ~ "Durisdinium",
      gen == "ORAPG" ~ "Durisdinium",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# make factors
infect <- infect %>%
  mutate(
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("control", "infected")),
    gen = factor(gen, levels = c("ECAH", "ECG", "ORAPG")),
    algae_type = factor(algae_type, levels = c("Cladocopium", "Durisdinium"))
  )

# interaction term
infect$treat_algae_interact <- interaction(infect$treatment, infect$gen)

# survival fit objects
infect_sfit <- survfit(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treat_algae_interact, data = infect)
summary(infect_sfit)$table

infect_sfit_treatment <- survfit(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treatment, data = infect)
infect_sfit_gen <- survfit(Surv(day, mortality) ~ gen, data = infect)

# Cox model
str(infect)
cox_poc <- coxph(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treat_algae_interact, data = infect)
summary(cox_poc)

# simplified model without interaction
simple_cox <- coxph(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treatment * gen, data = infect)
summary(simple_cox)

# check for zero or near-zero variance predictors
nearZeroVar(infect)

# examine number of events per group
table(infect$treatment, infect$mortality)
table(infect$gen, infect$mortality)

# if interaction is essential, consider stratification as an alternative approach
strat_cox <- coxph(Surv(day, mortality) ~ treatment + strata(gen), data = infect)
summary(strat_cox)

# Plotting the survival curves for treatment, faceted by genotype
p <- ggsurvplot(
  infect_sfit_treatment,
  data = infect,
  pval = TRUE,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  palette = c("grey1", "goldenrod1"),
  xlab = "Days",
  ylab = "Survival Probability",
  facet.by = "gen",
  facet.scales = "free_y"
)

p

ggsave("survival_plot_by_genotype.pdf", plot = print(p), height = 4, width = 6)
```
